seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in Amazon Bedrock Agents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in Amazon Bedrock Agents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Amazon Bedrock Agents, tool loops manifest as agents repeatedly invoking the same action group Lambda functions or querying knowledge bases without progressing toward task completion, often due to ambiguous instructions or malformed Lambda responses lacking completion signals. This creates excessive API calls, inflated costs, and stalled sessions in multi-turn interactions. Bedrock's session state management exacerbates the issue when agents fail to escape repetitive tool call patterns across invocations.

howItManifests.scenario: Action group Lambda returns incomplete parseable response
howItManifests.symptom: Agent repeatedly invokes same actionGroup with identical input
howItManifests.impact: 100+ API calls per session, $0.45+ per loop, session timeout

howItManifests.scenario: Knowledge base RAG returns ambiguous chunks triggering re-query
howItManifests.symptom: kbRetrieval tool called 5+ consecutive times with similar queries
howItManifests.impact: Incomplete answers, vector store overload, user frustration

howItManifests.scenario: Guardrails block final response, forcing tool retry
howItManifests.symptom: Final response blocked → actionGroup invoked → repeat
howItManifests.impact: Policy compliance but infinite loop, elevated guardrail costs

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-turn session state loses loop-breaking context
howItManifests.symptom: Agent forgets previous tool results, re-invokes tools
howItManifests.impact: Lost conversation context, degraded user experience

detection.name: Action Group Call Frequency Monitor
detection.description: Tracks consecutive invocations of the same actionGroup within session

detection.name: Knowledge Base Query Pattern Detector
detection.description: Detects repeated KB queries with semantic similarity > 0.9

detection.name: Session State Loop Tracer
detection.description: TypeScript monitor for Bedrock Agent session state drift

fixes.name: Lambda Completion Signal Fix
fixes.description: Force Lambda to return explicit completion with finalAnswer

fixes.name: Agent Instruction Loop Breaker
fixes.description: Add explicit loop prevention to agent instructions
fixes.code:
agent_instruction = """
You are a helpful assistant. IMPORTANT LOOP PREVENTION RULES:
1. Never call the same tool 3+ times consecutively without new user input
2. If you've called a tool twice with similar parameters, provide final answer
3. Always check if you have enough information before additional tool calls
4. Use 'finalAnswer' format when task is complete: {{finalAnswer}}Task complete{{/finalAnswer}}
"""

response = bedrock_agent_runtime.invoke_agent(
    agentId='YOUR_AGENT_ID',
    agentAliasId='YOUR_ALIAS_ID',
    sessionId=session_id,
    inputText=user_input,
    enableTrace=True,
    agentConfiguration={'instructions': agent_instruction}
)

fixes.name: Session State Persistence Wrapper
fixes.description: TypeScript wrapper maintains loop-breaking context across invocations

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade observability for Bedrock Agents by wrapping invoke_agent() calls with vex.verify() post-processing, automatically detecting tool loop patterns across action groups, knowledge bases, and session state. The platform maintains session-level traces spanning multiple Lambda invocations and API calls, identifying loops by actionGroup frequency, query similarity, and session duration anomalies.

Vex's ML-powered detectors flag loops in real-time with 98% precision, providing root cause analysis linking Lambda response formats to agent behavior. Automated remediation includes injecting loop-breaking instructions and enforcing max tool call limits per session. Integration takes <5 minutes with pip install vex-sdk.

The Vex dashboard surfaces loop patterns by agent alias, action group, and session duration, enabling teams to optimize Lambda functions and agent instructions at scale across enterprise deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
import boto3

# Install: pip install boto3 vex-sdk
bedrock_agent_runtime = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

@vex.verify(
    detectors=[
        'tool-loop-frequency',
        'kb-query-similarity',
        'session-duration-anomaly'
    ],
    remediation='inject-loop-breaker'
)
def invoke_bedrock_agent_with_protection(
    agent_id: str,
    session_id: str,
    input_text: str
) -> dict:
    """Vex-wrapped Bedrock Agent invocation with automatic loop detection"""
    
    response = bedrock_agent_runtime.invoke_agent(
        agentId=agent_id,
        agentAliasId='TSTALIASID',
        sessionId=session_id,
        inputText=input_text,
        enableTrace=True,
        allowInterruptions=True
    )
    
    # Vex automatically extracts trace, detects loops, applies fixes
    return {
        'response': response,
        'sessionId': session_id,
        'trace': response.get('completion', {}).get('trace')
    }

# Usage
result = invoke_bedrock_agent_with_protection(
    agent_id='YOUR_AGENT_ID',
    session_id='unique-session-123',
    input_text='Analyze sales data'
)
